docs.title: Handling errors in ASP.NET Core Web API
docs.text: Error handling is an essential aspect of building robust and reliable web APIs. ASP.NET Core provides various techniques to handle errors gracefully, ensuring a smooth experience for users and developers alike.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: In this article, we'll explore different approaches to handle errors in ASP.NET Core Web API applications. We'll cover built-in features and best practices for error handling.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
1. Introduction to Error Handling in ASP.NET Core
  2. Exception Handling Middleware
  3. Problem Details Middleware
  4. Logging Errors
  5. Conclusion

docs.title: Introduction to Error Handling in ASP.NET Core
docs.text: ASP.NET Core offers several options for handling errors, ranging from built-in middleware to custom error handling strategies. These mechanisms help developers manage exceptions, communicate errors effectively, and maintain application stability.

docs.title: Exception Handling Middleware
docs.text: Exception handling middleware intercepts unhandled exceptions and provides a centralized mechanism to process and respond to errors. By configuring exception handling middleware in the ASP.NET Core pipeline, developers can customize error responses, log exceptions, and maintain a consistent error handling strategy across the application.

docs.title: Problem Details Middleware
docs.text: Starting from ASP.NET Core 6.0, the framework includes built-in support for Problem Details, a standardized format for representing error details in API responses. Problem Details middleware automatically converts exceptions into Problem Details responses, adhering to the RFC 7807 specification. This approach simplifies error handling and promotes interoperability by providing a uniform error format for clients to consume.

docs.title: Logging Errors
docs.text: Logging is an integral part of error handling, enabling developers to track and analyze application failures. ASP.NET Core offers a flexible logging framework that integrates seamlessly with various logging providers. By logging error information, including exception details and contextual data, developers can diagnose issues, monitor application health, and troubleshoot errors efficiently.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
Effective error handling is crucial for building resilient ASP.NET Core Web APIs. By leveraging built-in features like exception handling middleware and Problem Details middleware, along with robust logging practices, developers can create APIs that deliver a reliable and user-friendly experience.

For more information on error handling in ASP.NET Core, refer to the official Microsoft documentation.
